text: Nephron sparing surgery is currently the standard treatment for CT-A renal tumors and minimal invasive surgery is in vogue. Robotic assistant has an added advantage over laparoscopy primarily in the excision, suturing and renal reconstruction but it adds to the cost. Increasing robotic use mandates innovative solutions to optimize the OR time. increasing the turnover and offsetting the cost. Our patient was a sixty seven year old gentleman, known diabetic, hypertensive, with chronic kidney disease and baseline serum creatinine of one point seven. Incidentally found to have three point nine into three centimeter left lower polar renal mass. CT scan was suggestive of renal mass. On functional scan the GFR of left kidney was twenty three point six ml per minute and of right kidney was twenty seven ml per minute.

The surgeon begins the left laparoscopy-assisted robotic partial nephrectomy by reflecting the left colon medially using a harmonic scalpel. Six robotic ports are utilized, with adapters converting the eight mm ports to five mm for compatibility with laparoscopic instruments.

The surgeon divides the lino renal ligament using a harmonic device to separate the kidney from the spleen. After identifying the gonadal vein, the colonic mesentery is reflected medially, and the ureterogonadal vein complex is lifted anterior to the swash sheath. Small vessels encountered are clipped and divided with the harmonic. The renal hilum is reached, and the renal vein is identified, dissected, and skeletonized using blunt and sharp dissection. The upper pole is further mobilized within the swast muscle using the harmonic.

The surgeon mobilizes the kidney posteriorly and laterally, completely separating it from the spleen. The hilum is further dissected, with the lumbar vein clipped and divided. The renal artery is identified posterior to the renal vein and dissected using blunt and sharp techniques.

The surgeon incises the peritumoral serotas, fascia, and fat using hook cautery until reaching the renal capsule, delineating the exophytic tumor. The renal artery is further dissected and skeletonized using blunt and sharp dissection. The Da Vinci SI robot is docked, and robotic bulldog clamps are applied to the renal artery and vein separately, allowing for tumor resection in a bloodless field.

The surgeon repairs the opened pelvic lysol system using PDS-IV-O suture, performing internal suturing with V-Loc to address bleeding points. The suture is brought out at the opposite surface of the kidney. Interrupted renal raphe is performed over a surgical bolster, securing hemostasis with Surgiflow. Renal vessels are de-clamped, and hemostasis is reconfirmed. Pirenal fat is sutured over the resected area, and the kidney is pegged to the abdominal wall. A drain is placed, and the specimen is retrieved using an endo-catch bag from a lateral port incision.

Total operative duration was two hundred minutes including laparoscopic console time of eighty minutes and robotic console time of sixty minutes. Warm ischemia time was twenty-four minutes fifteen seconds and the blood loss was less than fifty mL per minute. Histopathology revealed papillary carcinoma with clear margins. To conclude, hybridized laparoscopy assisted robotic partial nephrectomy consistently achieve an output of three cases in optimal times in ROR setup, optimizing the results, reducing times, increasing outputs and balancing surgeon's fatigue. The qualitative benefits accrue in terms of efficiency, reduce surgeon and team fatigue as well as preserving the minimal invasive benefits. Thank you.
